raw_text:
All narrow follow-up searches also returned no evidence. I now have everything needed to produce the final report.

---

# Variance Commentary — January 2026

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

January 2026 produced one significant variance against budget. **Sales revenue exceeded budget by £5,121 (+1,024.1%)**, with actual receipts of £5,621 against a budget of £500. This is a highly material positive variance representing more than ten times the budgeted revenue for the period. Despite exhaustive searches across CRM, email, Slack, and Calendar — both broad and narrow — no supporting context was retrievable for the period. The underlying driver cannot be confirmed from available sources and **requires urgent management review** to determine whether the variance reflects a genuine one-off transaction, a timing/recognition matter, or a budgeting error. All other line items are immaterial and require no executive attention.

---

## LINE COMMENTARY

### Sales
**Actual: £5,621 | Budget: £500 | Variance: £5,121 favourable (+1,024.1%)**

Sales revenue was £5,121 ahead of budget in January 2026, representing a 10x overperformance against the £500 budget. The magnitude of this variance suggests either: (a) one or more unbudgeted sales transactions closed in the period; (b) revenue was brought forward from a later period; or (c) the budget figure of £500 may itself be understated or a placeholder. No context found — recommend review.

Searches were conducted across all four available sources (CRM, Gmail, Slack, and Calendar) using both broad and narrow queries. All eight searches returned a `period_not_found` error, indicating a systemic data availability gap for January 2026 in the connected systems. This absence of evidence should itself be flagged, as it suggests either a data pipeline issue or that the relevant transactions/communications fall outside the indexed sources.

**Recommended next steps:**
1. Obtain the underlying sales ledger or invoicing detail to identify the specific transaction(s).
2. Confirm with the sales team whether a deal closed in January was not reflected in the budget.
3. Verify CRM and email system connectivity for the January 2026 date range to rule out a data ingestion failure.

#### Sources
- CRM — No evidence retrieved (broad & narrow) — `period_not_found` error returned for 2026-01-01 to 2026-01-31
- Gmail — No evidence retrieved (broad & narrow) — `period_not_found` error returned for 2026-01-01 to 2026-01-31
- Slack — No evidence retrieved (broad & narrow) — `period_not_found` error returned for 2026-01-01 to 2026-01-31
- Calendar — No evidence retrieved (broad & narrow) — `period_not_found` error returned for 2026-01-01 to 2026-01-31

---

## INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES

The following line items have been reviewed and determined to be immaterial. No further commentary is warranted.

| Line Item | Variance | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Audit & Accountancy Fees | £47 unfav. | Negligible |
| Bank Fees | £15 unfav. | Negligible |
| Entertainment — 100% Business | £13 unfav. | Negligible |
| General Expenses | £308 unfav. | Negligible |
| Light, Power & Heating | £49 unfav. (+61.7%) | Percentage elevated but absolute value immaterial |
| Motor Vehicle Expenses | £124 unfav. | Negligible |
| Printing & Stationery | £18 unfav. | Negligible |
| Rent | £984 unfav. | In line with budget (0.0% variance) |
| Subscriptions | £1,220 unfav. | In line with budget (0.0% variance) |
| Telephone & Internet | £37 unfav. | Negligible |
| Travel National | £29 unfav. | Negligible |

> **Note on Light, Power & Heating:** While the +61.7% variance rate is the highest percentage movement among insignificant lines, the absolute overspend of £49 is immaterial. No further investigation is required unless the trend persists into February 2026.
